title: Gothofreda guilleminiana
intro: Gothofreda guilleminiana, also known as 'Tetradenia rutilans', is a medicinal plant native to tropical Africa, belonging to the family Lamiaceae and commonly referred to as 'Tetradenia'. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Gothofreda guilleminiana include sesquiterpenoids, such as guillamin and guillamidine, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations, including infusions and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into various local remedies, such as cough syrups and expectorants.
